characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: AcCENT upon the Wrong SylLABle: Sometimes he gets so angry that he forgets how to pronounce words, a notable example coming from Season 8's third dinner service.Raj:Chef,we ran out of the sole special.Gordon:What? I've got three on or-DAAAAAAAAAAHR!! Actually Pretty Funny:As long as it's not during service, Gordon will usually laugh at the contestant's jokes or antics, usually during challenges. Only four times he actually found something funny during service, during Seasons 1, 5 and 6: The first was when Ralph praised Dewberry by calling him "[Scott's] diamond; my Gibraltar" to which Dewberry responded with: "I would rather you be calling me 'Brad Pitt's wife'". The second was when Michael suddenly yelled "You cook like old people fuck!" out of nowhere to his team during the first season finale. The third was when Robert split the back of his pants and continued cooking with his underwear exposed, and the fourth time was when Jean-Philippe told him there was a language barrier between him and Van because Van was from Texas.Gordon:(to Van and Jean-Philippe)Hey, what's the matter between you two?Jean-Philippe:There's a language barrier.Gordon:What do you mean, "there's a language barrier?" He's speaking English, you dick.Jean-Philippe:I know, but he's from Texas.(Gordon turns away, trying to stop himself from smiling)During one of Season 15's challenges, one of the contestants, Frank, made a dish with not enough potatoes in the plate. He explained he didn't want to put too many potatoes, causing Chef Ramsay to crack up. As long as it's not during service, Gordon will usually laugh at the contestant's jokes or antics, usually during challenges. characters.name: Scott Leibfried
characters.description: Badass Arm-Fold: He does as good a job of it as Chef Ramsay. Berserk Button:He tears Andrew Bonito a new one after catching him attempting to evade his team's punishment (having a combination lock put on the door to their storage room) by putting tape on the inside of the door, preventing it from fully closing, furiouslyreiterating that his team was punished because they screwed up, and they must suffer the consequences as opposed to attempting to rig them in their favorbefore taking all the chicken they'd brought to the kitchen back to the storeroom so they'd have to retrieve it again.Contestants who try to take control of the kitchen whenever Ramsay's not around. Just ask Benjamin Knack.Hereallyhates getting reamed by the contestants when they're in control of the kitchen. He got really pissed at Andrea when she got to temporarily run the kitchen and constantly criticized his work endlessly due to overconfidence and expecting all of his dishes to be sabotaged instead of just one or two dishes. It got to the point that he actually insulted and threatened her and thanked Chef Ramsay with relief when he took over again. He tears Andrew Bonito a new one after catching him attempting to evade his team's punishment (having a combination lock put on the door to their storage room) by putting tape on the inside of the door, preventing it from fully closing, furiouslyreiterating that his team was punished because they screwed up, and they must suffer the consequences as opposed to attempting to rig them in their favorbefore taking all the chicken they'd brought to the kitchen back to the storeroom so they'd have to retrieve it again. Contestants who try to take control of the kitchen whenever Ramsay's not around. Just ask Benjamin Knack. Hereallyhates getting reamed by the contestants when they're in control of the kitchen. He got really pissed at Andrea when she got to temporarily run the kitchen and constantly criticized his work endlessly due to overconfidence and expec

characters.name: James Avery
characters.description: The Bus Came Back: After being away for Seasons 15-22, he returns for Season 23. The Gadfly: During punishments that involve the blue team eating or drinking something really horrible, he often has a huge grin on his face. Hidden Depths: Along with Andi, he revealed that he's had to make a lot of sacrifices to be a chef and be as successful as he had and that it's really hard to balance work and his personal life with his wife and kids. Put on a Bus: Left in Season 15. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: Like Scott, he's big, bald, and someone who you definitely don't want to get on the wrong side of.

characters.name: Aaron Mitrano
characters.description: Beware the Quiet Ones: While generally more quieter and friendlier than someone like Scott, during the blue team’s punishment in Season 15 episode 12, he is seen by getting annoyed at Frank constantly chattering and boasting about his life in the marines instead of focusing on doing his job. He gets more dissatisfied again during the first dinner service in Season 16 episode 1, with the blue team’s dinner service being a total disaster he comes in the dorms, orders the blue team to return to the kitchen and answers a firm no to Johnny after the latter asks if he can quickly run back to the dorm to put his chef jacket back on. Out of Focus: Doesn't really get much screen time, even when compared to James before him. The Quiet One: Unlike the previous sous chefs on the blue team that appeared before him, Aaron is usually seen as more of reserved and quieter while still following Chef’s Ramsay’s standards. The Stoic: Is one of the most quietest sous chefs on the blue team to date, as well as being the most focused on the job at hand. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: Like Scott and James before him, he'sa third big bald manthat you don't want to get on the wrong side of.

characters.name: James "Jocky" Petrie
characters.description: Beware the Nice Ones: While for the most part he's much more laid-back than his three predecessors, he didn't hesitate to call out Jen on her attitude during Season 18. Hidden Depths: He showed some soccer skills during the All-Stars blue team's second reward when they competed with soccer Olympians. The Stoic: Much more calm and softly-spoken compared to his three predecessors, for the most part. Though he still doesn't hesitate to call out mistakes when he sees them being made.

characters.name: MaryAnnSalcedo
characters.description: Consummate Professional: Deconstructed. One of the main reasons she left the show is due to her annoyance having to work with unprofessional and unskilled contestants. Put on a Bus: Left after the third season, just as the show was starting to become more popular. Red Oni, Blue Oni: Ironically, she's the calmer Blue Oni to Chef Scott's aggressive Red Oni.

characters.name: Gloria Felix
characters.description: Put on a Bus: Was supposed to remain in her role for Seasons 6 and 7, but either quit or was fired at a late stage. The Silent Bob: Very rarely said or did anything of note while on the show, and usually just stood by the pass looking vaguely annoyed.

characters.name: Andi van Willigan
characters.description: Beware the Quiet Ones: Unlike Scott or Ramsay, Andi tends to be the quieter of the brigade; she's stoic but rarely shouts. Until you get her inMommie Dearest-mode. Oh boy. In the few instances she's become annoyed, she'd easily give Scott a run for his money, as Season 12's Anton can attest. The Bus Came Back: Returns as Sous Chef in Season 16. She leaves again next season, where Christina replaces the following season onwards. Character Focus: Since Scott Liebfried left after Season 10, Andi has been getting more focus out of herself and Sous Chefs James and Aaron. During black team services, she's usually the one helping out Chef Ramsay on service and helping test the chefs when they get to run the pass. A Day in the Limelight: Gets a noticeable amount of screen time in Season 12, arguably more so than Scott did during his time. She tells the red team herHidden Depths(see below) during one of their punishments, having an episode at Anton, giving Scott (the contestant) advice when he asks her for some, escorts a winning black jacket contestant to their reward, and tries to convince Joy from quitting in the middle of an ongoing service. Deadpan Snarker: She doesn't have many moments, but she can snark with the best of them.

characters.name: Jean-Philippe Susilovic
characters.description: Berserk Button: He really doesn't like Francisco, the party planner. Beware the Nice Ones: There are times where people push him too far, and he pushes back. One of the best examples of this is his tussle with Van in Episode 2 of Season 6. Butt-Monkey: Often has to deal with irritated customers due to the failures in the kitchen and theEpic Failof some of the contestants. That and Chef Ramsay loves picking on him. Fully morphed into this when he was forced to sumo wrestle with Ramsay. Camp Straight: Often mistaken forCamp Gayby viewers and even some of the contestants, but he confirmed to being heterosexual when indirectly questioned by Season 7's Red Team, and can occasionally be seen flirting with female diners. Commuting on a Bus: Didn't appear from Season 8 until Season 11. It turned out that he was busy opening Gordon's restaurant, Petrus by Gordon Ramsay. He returned for Seasons 11 and 12, only to leaveagainfor Season 13 to move back to his native Belgium.

characters.name: Francisco the Party Planner
characters.description: Blithe Spirit: He's constantly happy and having a good time...even when other competitors get annoyed by him. Camp Gay: Oh so much. LA even refers to him as "parade gay". Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: After the fifth season, he never made an appearance again. Sitcom Arch-Nemesis: To Jean-Philippe. Every time Francisco shows up, expect JP to get frustrated with him.

characters.name: James Lukanik
characters.description: Funny Foreigner: Actually born in the UK, though to Eastern European parents. Get a Hold of Yourself, Man!: Does this for Brian when he's kicked out of the kitchen and states that Chef Ramsay needs him to fight back. Brian listens. Suspiciously Similar Substitute:Funny ForeignerMaître d' who's a bit uptight?

characters.name: Marino Monferrato
characters.description: Ascended Extra: Appeared as a challenge judge in Season 11, before taking over as the show's maître d' two seasons later. Butt-Monkey: Subverted. Oftentimes, Ramsay will poke fun at him, as he did with Jean-Philippe, but it usually results in Marino responding by firing back at Ramsay. For example, in Season 22, when Ramsay threw food at him as a joke, Marino immediately did it back in good fun, thus starting a food fight. He also often enjoys doing silly things rather than being the butt of the joke itself, such as appearing in ridiculous costumes like a cowboy or a space suit. Catchphrase: He often saysSi, chef!in place of the usual "Yes, chef!" Deadpan Snarker: He gets to indulge in this in Season 20, snarking at the chefsEpic Failmoments during service.Marino: (During Chickengate when he sees the raw chicken) Come on guys, even the front of house guy can cook it better.Marino: (After returning Megan Ramsay's bland noodles) It tasted nothing like the one she had this morning, so go back to your drawing boards. Funny Foreigner: Marino is Italian and peppers Italian in when he's speaking. He also gets to indulge in antics that JP would never have gotten away with, like trying to catch burnt food that Chef Ramsay was throwing in his direction.

characters.name: Matthew Childs
characters.description: Funny Foreigner: Averted; Matthew is the first American maître d' in the show's history. Temporary Substitute: Fills in the maître d' role while Marino is away visiting family.

characters.name: Jason Thompson
characters.description: The Bus Came Back: Returned in Season 15. Deadpan Snarker: Most of his commentaries come off very ironic in context. Everyone Calls Him "Barkeep": Even many longtime fans of the show don't even know his name and just refer to him as "the announcer". The Faceless: He is never seen. Pungeon Master: Too many instances to count.

characters.name: Roger Craig Smith
characters.description: Yes,thatRogerCraigSmith. The Voice: Just like Jason, he's only known as such.
